title: amqplib connect with rejectUnauthorized: false — the RabbitMQ broker certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: The amqplib connection validates the broker certificate (rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the amqps:// connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: amqplib connect is called with rejectUnauthorized: false in its socket options, disabling validation of the RabbitMQ broker's TLS certificate. A network attacker can present a forged certificate and man-in-the-middle the connection — reading and injecting messages on every queue (jobs, events, credentials). Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the broker CA via the `ca` socket option.
params.absentCallDetail: No amqplib connect call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: amqplib connect does not set rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: rejectUnauthorized: false is pasted to connect to a RabbitMQ broker with a self-signed cert and ships, exposing the job/event bus to MITM.
